records.summary: Expandable accordion component that renders a list of collapsible content sections with animated transitions, single or multiple selection, and three style variants.
whenToUse: Use when presenting a list of FAQ-style sections or collapsible categories.
Use when screen real estate is limited and content can be progressively disclosed.
Use for settings pages, help content, or grouped information.
Use when content sections need single or multiple open state management.
whenNotToUse: Do not use for tab-style navigation where all sections are equally accessible.
Do not use when content is short enough to be shown without collapsing.
Do not use for navigation between separate screens; use navigation primitives instead.

props.name: items
props.description: Array of accordion items, each with id, title, and content (string or ReactNode).

props.name: value
props.description: Controlled expanded item id(s). Use with onChange for controlled usage.

props.name: defaultValue
props.description: Default expanded item id(s) for uncontrolled usage.

props.name: onChange
props.description: Callback fired when an item is expanded or collapsed.

props.name: collapsible
props.description: When true, allows all items to be collapsed. When false, at least one item stays open.

props.name: multiple
props.description: When true, allows multiple items to be open simultaneously.

props.name: variant
props.description: Visual style variant. default: white background, filled: gray[50] background, outline: bordered.

variants.name: default
variants.description: White background, no border on the item header.

variants.name: filled
variants.description: Gray[50] background on header and content.

variants.name: outline
variants.description: White background with a gray[200] border around header and content.

notes: Uses theme.colors for header background and text colors based on variant.

examples.title: Basic uncontrolled accordion
examples.description: Simple accordion with three items, collapsible, single selection.

examples.title: Controlled accordion
examples.description: Controlled accordion with external state management.

examples.title: Multiple selection accordion
examples.description: Allow multiple items open at once.

examples.title: Outline variant
examples.description: Bordered outline style.
examples.code:
import { Accordion } from 'fluent-styles';

<Accordion items={items} variant="outline" />

bestPractices: Provide unique stable string ids for each AccordionItemConfig item.
Use defaultValue for uncontrolled and value+onChange for controlled usage.
Prefer ReactNode content over plain strings for rich section content.
Use multiple={true} only when sections are truly independent.
antiPatterns: Do not set multiple={true} and supply a string defaultValue — use an array.
Do not use Accordion for primary navigation; use tab or drawer navigation.
generationHints: Import Accordion from 'fluent-styles'.
Always provide items as AccordionItemConfig[] with unique ids.
Use defaultValue to open items by default in uncontrolled mode.
Use variant='filled' for settings-style accordions.
Use variant='outline' for card-like bordered accordions.

typeDefinitions.name: AccordionProps
typeDefinitions.summary: Props for the Accordion component including items, controlled state, and variant.

typeDefinitions.name: AccordionItemConfig
typeDefinitions.signature: { id: string; title: string; content: ReactNode | string }
typeDefinitions.summary: Configuration object for each accordion item.

records.embeddingText: Accordion is a fluent-styles collapsible content component. It renders a list of expandable sections with animated transitions. Supports default, filled, and outline variants. Supports single and multiple selection. Accepts items as AccordionItemConfig[]. Export confirmed in src/index.ts.
records.rawContent: Component Accordion. Category navigation. Exported from fluent-styles via src/index.ts. Source: src/package/accordion/index.tsx. Expandable accordion with variants: default, filled, outline. Multiple and collapsible modes. Related: Pressable, StyledText, YStack.

metadata.notes: Fully verified from source. Props, variants, and examples grounded in source code and Storybook stories.

records.summary: Checkbox input component with controlled/uncontrolled state, customizable colors, flexible sizing, disabled state, and MaterialIcons checkmark.
whenToUse: Use for boolean form fields like accept terms, enable feature, multi-select options.
Use when users need to toggle individual options independently.
Use in lists where multiple items can be selected simultaneously.
whenNotToUse: Do not use CheckBox for mutually exclusive single-select options; use RadioButton instead.
Do not use when a Switch better communicates an on/off state with immediate effect.

props.name: checked
props.description: Controlled checked state.

props.name: onCheck
props.description: Callback fired with the new checked state when the checkbox is pressed.

props.name: checkedColor
props.description: Background and border color when checked.

props.name: uncheckedColor
props.description: Background color when unchecked.

props.name: borderColor
props.description: Border color override.

props.name: checkMarkColor
props.description: Color of the checkmark icon.

props.name: size
props.description: Width and height of the checkbox in pixels.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables interaction and reduces opacity to 0.6.

props.name: iconName
props.description: MaterialIcons icon name for the checkmark.

props.name: iconSize
props.description: Size of the checkmark icon.

props.name: iconProps
props.description: Additional props passed to the MaterialIcons component.

variants.name: unchecked
variants.description: Gray[50] background, gray[300] border, no icon visible.

variants.name: checked
variants.description: Blue[500] background and border, white checkmark icon.

notes: Checked state uses blue[500] by default, overridable via checkedColor.

examples.title: Uncontrolled checkbox
examples.description: Basic checkbox with callback.

examples.title: Controlled checkbox
examples.description: Controlled checkbox with external state.

examples.title: Custom color checkbox
examples.description: Checkbox with custom brand color.

examples.title: Disabled checkbox
examples.description: Checkbox that cannot be interacted with.
examples.code:
import { CheckBox } from 'fluent-styles';

<CheckBox checked={true} disabled={true} />

bestPractices: Use checked + onCheck for controlled form integration.
Always provide accessible label via the parent or accessibilityLabel.
Use checkedColor to match brand primary color when needed.
antiPatterns: Do not use CheckBox for mutually exclusive options; use RadioButton.
Do not rely solely on color to communicate state for accessibility.
generationHints: Import CheckBox from 'fluent-styles'.
Wrap in XStack with StyledText for labeled checkbox rows.
Use checked + onCheck for controlled form state.
Default checked color is blue[500]; override with checkedColor.

typeDefinitions.name: CheckBoxProps
typeDefinitions.summary: Props for the CheckBox component.

records.embeddingText: CheckBox is a fluent-styles form component for boolean toggle input. Supports controlled and uncontrolled state, customizable colors, size, disabled state. Uses MaterialIcons check icon. Export confirmed in src/index.ts.
records.rawContent: Component CheckBox. Category form. Exported from fluent-styles via src/index.ts. Source: src/package/checkBox/index.tsx. Checkbox with checked, onCheck, checkedColor, size, disabled props. Related: RadioButton, Switch.

metadata.notes: Fully verified from source. All props extracted directly from CheckBoxProps interface.

records.summary: Screen header family consisting of StyledHeader (status-bar-aware container), Header (title + back icon row), and Full (passthrough wrapper). Handles iOS/Android status bar height automatically.
whenToUse: Use StyledHeader as the top-level header container to get correct status bar padding.
Use Header for screens that need a back button, title, and optional right action.
Use Full when you need a custom header layout inside StyledHeader without the default Header row.
whenNotToUse: Do not use StyledHeader if you are using a navigation library header (React Navigation) — they conflict.
Do not use Header without StyledHeader wrapper if status bar height compensation is needed.

props.name: children
props.description: StyledHeader: Content rendered below the status bar. Header: Not applicable.

props.name: statusProps
props.description: StyledHeader: Props forwarded to React Native StatusBar. Includes barStyle, backgroundColor, translucent, animated, hidden.

props.name: skipAndroid
props.description: StyledHeader: Skip status bar height offset on Android.

props.name: skipIos
props.description: StyledHeader: Skip status bar height offset on iOS.

props.name: title
props.description: Header: Title text rendered in the header row.

props.name: icon
props.description: Header: When true, renders a back arrow icon (MaterialIcons arrow-back) before the title.

props.name: onPress
props.description: Header: Callback for the back icon press.

props.name: rightIcon
props.description: Header: Optional ReactNode rendered on the right side of the header.

props.name: color
props.description: Header: Title text color.

props.name: fontSize
props.description: Header: Title font size.

props.name: fontWeight
props.description: Header: Title font weight.

props.name: cycleProps
props.description: Header: Props forwarded to the StyledCycle container wrapping the back icon.

props.name: textProps
props.description: Header: Additional props passed to StyledText for the title.

variants.name: StyledHeader
variants.description: Top-level status-bar-aware header container. Wraps children with StatusBar and correct top padding.

variants.name: Header
variants.description: Content row with optional back icon, title, and right icon slot.

variants.name: Full
variants.description: Passthrough wrapper for fully custom header content inside StyledHeader.

notes: Status bar defaults to dark-content with gray[1] backgroundColor.

examples.title: Basic header with back button and title
examples.description: Screen header with back navigation and title.

examples.title: Header with right action
examples.description: Header with back button, title, and a right-side icon.

examples.title: Custom status bar style
examples.description: Header with light content status bar for dark backgrounds.
examples.code:
import { StyledHeader, Header } from 'fluent-styles';

<StyledHeader statusProps={{ barStyle: 'light-content', backgroundColor: '#1e293b' }}>
  <Header title="Dashboard" />
</StyledHeader>

bestPractices: Always wrap Header inside StyledHeader to get correct status bar height.
Use skipAndroid={false} when targeting Android with custom status bar height.
Provide onPress when icon={true} to handle back navigation.
antiPatterns: Do not use StyledHeader alongside React Navigation's own header — disable one.
Do not pass statusBar height manually; use StyledHeader's skipAndroid/skipIos instead.
generationHints: Import StyledHeader and Header from 'fluent-styles'.
Wrap Header inside StyledHeader for proper status bar handling.
Use icon={true} with onPress for back navigation.
Use rightIcon prop to add action icons to the right side.
Use Full inside StyledHeader for fully custom header layouts.

typeDefinitions.name: StyledHeaderProps
typeDefinitions.signature: { children?: ReactNode; statusProps?: StatusBarProps; skipAndroid?: boolean; skipIos?: boolean }
typeDefinitions.summary: Props for the StyledHeader status-bar-aware container.

typeDefinitions.name: HeaderProps
typeDefinitions.summary: Props for the Header content row.

records.embeddingText: StyledHeader is a fluent-styles navigation header family. StyledHeader handles status bar height. Header renders title, back icon, and right slot. Full is a passthrough wrapper. Export confirmed in src/index.ts.
records.rawContent: Component StyledHeader/Header/Full. Category navigation. Exported from fluent-styles via src/index.ts. Source: src/package/header/index.tsx. Status-bar-aware header. Header has title, icon (back button), onPress, rightIcon. Related: StyledCycle, StyledText, StyledIcon.

metadata.notes: Fully verified from source. StatusBar integration and StyledCycle back-icon usage confirmed.

records.summary: Styled MaterialIcons wrapper with named size variants (xs/sm/md/lg/xl), color theming, and optional press handlers. Wraps react-native-vector-icons/MaterialIcons.
whenToUse: Use StyledIcon wherever a Material Design icon is needed in the UI.
Use when you need consistent themed icon sizing across the app.
Use with onPress for icon buttons or touchable icon actions.
whenNotToUse: Do not use StyledIcon for icon-in-button use cases; prefer PressableIcon.
Do not use if you need icons from a different icon set (e.g. FontAwesome).

props.name: name
props.description: MaterialIcons icon name (e.g. 'settings', 'close', 'arrow-back').

props.name: size
props.description: Icon size. Named variants map to: xs=16, sm=20, md=24, lg=32, xl=48. Accepts raw number.

props.name: color
props.description: Icon color.

props.name: onPress
props.description: Fired when the icon is tapped.

props.name: onLongPress
props.description: Fired when the icon is long-pressed.

notes: Default color is theme.colors.gray[800]. Override with color prop.

examples.title: Basic icon
examples.description: Render a home icon at default size.
examples.code:
import { StyledIcon } from 'fluent-styles';

<StyledIcon name="home" />

examples.title: Icon with size and color
examples.description: Large icon with custom color.
examples.code:
import { StyledIcon } from 'fluent-styles';

<StyledIcon name="settings" size="lg" color="#3b82f6" />

examples.title: Pressable icon
examples.description: Tappable icon with press handler.
examples.code:
import { StyledIcon } from 'fluent-styles';

<StyledIcon name="close" size="md" onPress={() => onDismiss()} />

examples.title: Numeric size
examples.description: Icon with exact pixel size.
examples.code:
import { StyledIcon } from 'fluent-styles';

<StyledIcon name="star" size={36} color="#fbbf24" />

bestPractices: Use named size variants (xs/sm/md/lg/xl) over raw numbers for consistency.
Wrap StyledIcon in StyledCycle for circular icon containers.
Use PressableIcon when the icon needs pressable feedback and hit area.
antiPatterns: Do not use StyledIcon as a standalone button without proper hit area; use PressableIcon.
Do not use hardcoded colors; use theme tokens or pass color from context.
generationHints: Import StyledIcon from 'fluent-styles'.
Provide name as a MaterialIcons icon name string.
Use size='lg' or size='xl' for prominent decorative icons.
Wrap in StyledCycle for circular icon avatar style containers.

typeDefinitions.name: StyledIconProps
typeDefinitions.summary: Props for StyledIcon including name, size, color, and press handlers.

typeDefinitions.name: IconVariants
typeDefinitions.summary: Named size variants for StyledIcon.

records.embeddingText: StyledIcon wraps react-native-vector-icons MaterialIcons with fluent-styles size variants xs sm md lg xl and theme color defaults. Supports onPress and onLongPress. Export confirmed in src/index.ts.
records.rawContent: Component StyledIcon. Category helper. Exported from fluent-styles via src/index.ts. Source: src/package/icon/index.tsx. MaterialIcons wrapper with size variants. Related: PressableIcon, StyledCycle.

metadata.notes: Fully verified from source. Size variant map and color defaults extracted directly from source.

records.summary: Image family with automatic error fallback, local/remote source handling, and shape variants (rounded/square/cycle). Includes StyledImageBackground for background image containers.
whenToUse: Use StyledImage for all image rendering with built-in fallback handling.
Use when you need rounded, circular, or square image shapes via declarative props.
Use StyledImageBackground when image should serve as a container background.
Use local={true} when loading a local require() asset.
whenNotToUse: Do not use StyledImage for icons; use StyledIcon instead.
Do not use if you need advanced image loading libraries like react-native-fast-image.

props.name: imageUrl
props.description: Remote image URL. Falls back to fallback image on error or if not provided.

props.name: local
props.description: When true, treats imageUrl as a local require() asset path.

props.name: fallback
props.description: Fallback image source shown when imageUrl fails to load or is not provided.

props.name: rounded
props.description: Applies borderRadius: 8 for rounded corners.

props.name: square
props.description: Applies borderRadius: 0 for explicit square corners.

props.name: cycle
props.description: Applies borderRadius: 50 for a circular image shape.

props.name: children
props.description: StyledImageBackground only: Content rendered on top of the background image.

notes: resizeMode defaults to 'cover'. Fallback image is built-in.

examples.title: Remote image with fallback
examples.description: Load remote URL with automatic fallback.

examples.title: Circular avatar
examples.description: Circular user avatar with cycle variant.

examples.title: Rounded card image
examples.description: Product image with rounded corners.

examples.title: Background image container
examples.description: Full-width hero with content on top.
examples.code:
import { StyledImageBackground, StyledText } from 'fluent-styles';

<StyledImageBackground
  imageUrl="https://example.com/hero.jpg"
  style={{ width: '100%', height: 240 }}
>
  <StyledText color="#fff" fontSize={24} fontWeight="700">Welcome</StyledText>
</StyledImageBackground>

bestPractices: Always provide width and height via style prop; images have no intrinsic size in RN.
Use cycle={true} for consistent avatar shapes.
Provide a meaningful fallback for better UX on load failures.
antiPatterns: Do not use StyledImage without explicit dimensions — it will render invisible.
Do not use cycle variant without equal width and height — shape will be elliptical.
generationHints: Import StyledImage from 'fluent-styles'.
Always pass explicit width and height in the style prop.
Use cycle={true} for avatars.
Use rounded={true} for card product images.
Use StyledImageBackground for hero images with overlaid content.

typeDefinitions.name: StyledImageProps
typeDefinitions.summary: Props for StyledImage with imageUrl, local, fallback, and shape variants.

typeDefinitions.name: StyledImageBackgroundProps
typeDefinitions.summary: Props for StyledImageBackground, same as StyledImageProps plus children.

typeDefinitions.name: ImageVariants
typeDefinitions.signature: { rounded?: boolean; square?: boolean; cycle?: boolean }
typeDefinitions.summary: Shape variant props for image components.

records.embeddingText: StyledImage is a fluent-styles image component with automatic fallback, remote/local source handling, and shape variants: rounded, square, cycle. StyledImageBackground renders an image as a background container. Export confirmed in src/index.ts.
records.rawContent: Component StyledImage/StyledImageBackground. Category image. Exported from fluent-styles via src/index.ts. Source: src/package/image/index.tsx. Shape variants: rounded (8px), square, cycle (50px). Fallback image included.

metadata.notes: Fully verified from source. Variant logic, fallback logic, and StyledImageBackground confirmed.

records.summary: Keyboard avoidance family of four components: KeyboardAvoidingView (base), KeyboardAvoidingForm (form-optimized), KeyboardAvoidingPadding (explicit padding control), KeyboardAwareContent (renderContent helper). Auto-selects iOS/Android behavior.
whenToUse: Use KeyboardAvoidingView as the root container for any screen with text inputs.
Use KeyboardAvoidingForm when a screen has a group of form fields with a submit button at the bottom.
Use KeyboardAvoidingPadding when you need fine-grained padding-only control.
Use KeyboardAwareContent with renderContent for scroll-based content.
whenNotToUse: Do not wrap the entire app layout in KeyboardAvoidingView — use it at the screen level.
Do not use if the screen has no interactive text inputs.

props.name: variant
props.description: Background color preset. surface=white, light=gray[50], dark=gray[900], primary=blue[50], transparent.

props.name: padded
props.description: Padding preset. true=medium(16), small=8, medium=16, large=24.

props.name: behavior
props.description: RN KeyboardAvoidingView behavior. Auto-set per platform by default.

props.name: keyOffset
props.description: keyboardVerticalOffset passed to the native KeyboardAvoidingView.

props.name: backgroundColor
props.description: Override background color directly, bypassing variant.

props.name: flex
props.description: Flex value for the container.

props.name: justifyContent
props.description: Flex justifyContent for the container.

props.name: alignItems
props.description: Flex alignItems for the container.

props.name: accessibilityLabel
props.description: Accessibility label for the container.

props.name: onSubmit
props.description: KeyboardAvoidingForm only: Called when the last child (submit button) is pressed.

props.name: spacing
props.description: KeyboardAvoidingForm only: Gap between form fields.

props.name: renderContent
props.description: KeyboardAwareContent only: Render function for scrollable content.

variants.name: KeyboardAvoidingView
variants.description: Base container with variant, padded, behavior, keyOffset, flex, justifyContent, alignItems.

variants.name: KeyboardAvoidingForm
variants.description: Form-optimized: splits children into fields + last-child submit button. Adds spacing and onSubmit.

variants.name: KeyboardAwareContent
variants.description: Accepts renderContent prop for explicit content render.

notes: Variant presets map to theme colors. behavior auto-set per platform.

examples.title: Basic keyboard avoiding screen
examples.description: Wrap a screen with inputs to avoid keyboard overlap.
examples.code:
import { KeyboardAvoidingView } from 'fluent-styles';

<KeyboardAvoidingView variant="surface" padded="medium">
  <StyledInput label="Email" />
  <StyledInput label="Password" />
</KeyboardAvoidingView>

examples.title: Login form with submit button
examples.description: KeyboardAvoidingForm pushes submit to the bottom.
examples.code:
import { KeyboardAvoidingForm, StyledInput, StyledButton } from 'fluent-styles';

<KeyboardAvoidingForm
  variant="surface"
  padded="large"
  onSubmit={handleLogin}
>
  <StyledInput label="Email" />
  <StyledInput label="Password" />
  <StyledButton onPress={handleLogin}>Login</StyledButton>
</KeyboardAvoidingForm>

examples.title: Transparent overlay keyboard container
examples.description: Transparent keyboard avoidance for modal-style screens.

bestPractices: Use KeyboardAvoidingForm for login, signup, and settings screens with a submit button.
Set keyOffset on iOS when the screen has custom header heights.
Prefer variant presets over raw backgroundColor for theme consistency.
antiPatterns: Do not nest KeyboardAvoidingView inside a ScrollView — they conflict on Android.
Do not omit behavior — always rely on the auto-detect default or set explicitly per platform.
generationHints: Import KeyboardAvoidingView or KeyboardAvoidingForm from 'fluent-styles'.
Use KeyboardAvoidingForm for any screen with form fields + submit button.
The last child of KeyboardAvoidingForm is treated as the submit button.
Use padded='large' for forms to provide generous touch targets.
Default behavior is auto-detected for iOS (padding) and Android (height).

typeDefinitions.name: KeyboardAvoidingViewProps
typeDefinitions.summary: Props for the base KeyboardAvoidingView.

typeDefinitions.name: KeyboardAvoidingFormProps
typeDefinitions.summary: KeyboardAvoidingForm adds onSubmit and spacing.

records.embeddingText: KeyboardAvoidingView is a fluent-styles helper component family for keyboard avoidance. Auto-detects iOS/Android behavior. Includes KeyboardAvoidingForm for form+submit layouts, KeyboardAvoidingPadding for explicit padding, KeyboardAwareContent for renderContent. Export confirmed in src/index.ts.
records.rawContent: Component KeyboardAvoidingView/KeyboardAvoidingForm/KeyboardAvoidingPadding/KeyboardAwareContent. Category helper. Exported from fluent-styles via src/index.ts. Variants: light, dark, primary, surface, transparent. Padded: small/medium/large.

metadata.notes: Fully verified from source. Four member family confirmed. Platform-specific behavior auto-detect confirmed.

records.summary: Circular radio button input for single-select groups. Uses name/selected pattern for group membership. Customizable colors, size, and indicator icon. RadioGroupProps type supports group management.
whenToUse: Use RadioButton for mutually exclusive single-select form options.
Use when users must pick exactly one option from a fixed set.
Use for preference settings, payment methods, or plan selection.
whenNotToUse: Do not use RadioButton for independent toggles; use CheckBox instead.
Do not use when more than 5-6 options exist; consider a Dropdown.

props.name: name
props.description: Unique identifier for this radio button within a group.

props.name: selected
props.description: The currently selected radio button name in the group. RadioButton is selected when selected === name.

props.name: onSelect
props.description: Callback fired with the name of this button when pressed.

props.name: selectedColor
props.description: Background and border color when selected.

props.name: unselectedColor
props.description: Background color when unselected.

props.name: borderColor
props.description: Border color override.

props.name: indicatorColor
props.description: Color of the inner indicator dot.

props.name: size
props.description: Width and height of the radio button in pixels.

props.name: indicatorSize
props.description: Size of the inner indicator icon.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables interaction and reduces opacity.

props.name: iconName
props.description: MaterialIcons icon name used as the selection indicator.

variants.name: unselected
variants.description: Gray[50] background, gray[300] border, no indicator.

variants.name: selected
variants.description: Blue[500] background and border, white circle indicator.

notes: Selected state uses blue[500] by default; override with selectedColor.

examples.title: Radio group with state
examples.description: Basic radio button group with external state.
examples.code:
import { useState } from 'react';
import { RadioButton, XStack, StyledText } from 'fluent-styles';

const [plan, setPlan] = useState('monthly');

<XStack gap={16}>
  <XStack alignItems="center" gap={8}>
    <RadioButton name="monthly" selected={plan} onSelect={setPlan} />
    <StyledText>Monthly</StyledText>
  </XStack>
  <XStack alignItems="center" gap={8}>
    <RadioButton name="annual" selected={plan} onSelect={setPlan} />
    <StyledText>Annual</StyledText>
  </XStack>
</XStack>

examples.title: Custom color radio button
examples.description: Radio button with brand color.

bestPractices: Always manage selected state at the group level, not per RadioButton.
Wrap RadioButton + StyledText in XStack for accessible labeled radio rows.
Use the RadioGroupProps type to type your group state handler.
antiPatterns: Do not use RadioButton for independent toggles; use CheckBox.
Do not allow more than ~6 radio options; use Dropdown for long lists.
generationHints: Import RadioButton from 'fluent-styles'.
Manage selected state at group level; pass shared selected value to each RadioButton.
Each RadioButton needs a unique name prop.
Wrap RadioButton + label in XStack with gap={8} for inline labeled options.
Use YStack to stack radio options vertically.

typeDefinitions.name: RadioButtonProps
typeDefinitions.summary: Props for RadioButton including name, selected, onSelect, colors, and size.

typeDefinitions.name: RadioGroupProps
typeDefinitions.signature: { name: string; value?: string; onValueChange?: (value: string) => void; disabled?: boolean; children?: ReactNode }
typeDefinitions.summary: Props type for managing a RadioButton group.

records.embeddingText: RadioButton is a fluent-styles form component for single-select groups. Uses name/selected pattern. Blue[500] selected color. Customizable colors and size. Has RadioGroupProps type for group management. Export confirmed in src/index.ts.
records.rawContent: Component RadioButton. Category form. Exported from fluent-styles via src/index.ts. Source: src/package/radioButton/index.tsx. name/selected/onSelect pattern. Related: CheckBox, Dropdown.

metadata.notes: Fully verified from source. name/selected selection pattern confirmed.

records.summary: Toggle switch family: Switch (bare toggle), SwitchRow (labeled toggle with description), GroupedSwitch (bordered list of labeled switches). Supports size, variant, and controlled/uncontrolled state.
whenToUse: Use Switch for immediate on/off toggles (notifications, settings flags).
Use SwitchRow when each toggle needs a label and optional description.
Use GroupedSwitch for settings screens with multiple labeled toggles in a bordered group.
Use when the toggle effect is immediate (no submit needed).
whenNotToUse: Do not use Switch for multi-select options; use CheckBox.
Do not use when the toggle requires confirmation before applying; use a Dialog.

props.name: value
props.description: Current toggle state.

props.name: onValueChange
props.description: Callback fired with the new boolean state when toggled.

props.name: size
props.description: Scale of the switch. small=1.1x, medium=1.5x, large=2x native scale.

props.name: variant
props.description: Color variant for the on-state track and thumb. primary=blue[400/600], success=green, warning=amber, danger=red.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables interaction and reduces opacity to 0.5.

props.name: trackColorOff
props.description: Track color in off state.

props.name: trackColorOn
props.description: Track color in on state. Defaults to variant track color.

props.name: thumbColorOff
props.description: Thumb color in off state.

props.name: thumbColorOn
props.description: Thumb color in on state. Defaults to variant thumb color.

props.name: iosBackgroundColor
props.description: iOS-specific background color.

props.name: label
props.description: SwitchRow only: Label text rendered to the left of the toggle.

props.name: description
props.description: SwitchRow only: Secondary description text below the label.

props.name: switches
props.type: Array<{ id: string; label: string; description?: string; value: boolean }>
props.description: GroupedSwitch only: Array of switch configuration objects.

props.name: onChange
props.description: GroupedSwitch only: Callback fired with the changed switch id and new value.

notes: Switch scales native RN Switch. Variant maps directly to theme color tokens.

examples.title: Basic switch
examples.description: Simple on/off toggle.

examples.title: SwitchRow with label and description
examples.description: Labeled switch with secondary description.

examples.title: GroupedSwitch settings panel
examples.description: Multiple toggles in a bordered group.

examples.title: Danger variant switch
examples.description: Switch with red variant for destructive setting.

bestPractices: Use SwitchRow for all labeled toggles to ensure consistent spacing.
Use GroupedSwitch for settings sections with 2+ related toggles.
Always use variant to communicate semantic intent (success for enable, danger for destructive).
antiPatterns: Do not use Switch for form options that need explicit submission; use CheckBox.
Do not use multiple bare Switch components in a vertical list; use GroupedSwitch.
generationHints: Import Switch, SwitchRow, or GroupedSwitch from 'fluent-styles'.
Use SwitchRow for any switch that needs a label.
Use GroupedSwitch for a settings section with multiple toggles.
Default variant is 'primary' (blue); change to 'success' or 'danger' as needed.
GroupedSwitch wraps switches in a bordered group automatically.

typeDefinitions.name: SwitchProps
typeDefinitions.summary: Props for the bare Switch component.

typeDefinitions.name: SwitchRowProps
typeDefinitions.summary: SwitchRow adds label, description, labelColor, descriptionColor.

typeDefinitions.name: GroupedSwitchProps
typeDefinitions.summary: GroupedSwitch takes a switches array and onChange handler.

records.embeddingText: Switch is a fluent-styles form toggle family. Switch is bare toggle. SwitchRow adds label and description. GroupedSwitch renders a bordered settings group. Variants: default, primary, success, warning, danger. Sizes: small, medium, large. Export confirmed in src/index.ts.
records.rawContent: Component Switch/SwitchRow/GroupedSwitch. Category form. Exported from fluent-styles via src/index.ts. Source: src/package/switch/index.tsx. Color variants and size scale confirmed.

metadata.notes: Fully verified from source. Three-member family, variant config, and size scale confirmed.

records.summary: Circular container component for wrapping icons, avatars, or badges. Provides four named size variants (sm/md/lg/xl) with gray border and background by default. Used internally by Header for back-button containers.
whenToUse: Use StyledCycle to wrap StyledIcon components in a circular container.
Use for avatar containers, icon badges, or any circular UI element.
Use when you need consistent circular sizing across the app.
whenNotToUse: Do not use StyledCycle for rectangular or rounded containers; use StyledCard or YStack with borderRadius.
Do not use StyledCycle for images; use StyledImage with cycle={true} instead.

props.name: size
props.description: Named size variant. sm=32x32, md=48x48, lg=64x64, xl=80x80.

props.name: children
props.description: Content centered inside the circle.

notes: Default background is gray[50] with gray[200] border. Override via style or backgroundColor prop.

examples.title: Icon in circle
examples.description: Circular icon container for navigation or action.
examples.code:
import { StyledCycle, StyledIcon } from 'fluent-styles';

<StyledCycle size="md">
  <StyledIcon name="arrow-back" size="md" />
</StyledCycle>

examples.title: Large avatar placeholder
examples.description: XL cycle for user avatar display.
examples.code:
import { StyledCycle, StyledIcon } from 'fluent-styles';

<StyledCycle size="xl" backgroundColor="#e0e7ff">
  <StyledIcon name="person" size="xl" color="#4f46e5" />
</StyledCycle>

examples.title: Custom colored cycle
examples.description: Cycle with brand background.
examples.code:
import { StyledCycle, StyledIcon } from 'fluent-styles';

<StyledCycle size="lg" backgroundColor="#3b82f61a" borderColor="#3b82f6">
  <StyledIcon name="star" size="lg" color="#3b82f6" />
</StyledCycle>

bestPractices: Use size variant instead of raw width/height for consistent circular containers.
Pair with StyledIcon for icon containers.
Override backgroundColor and borderColor via style props for custom brand colors.
antiPatterns: Do not use for non-circular containers; use YStack with borderRadius instead.
Do not put multiple children inside StyledCycle; it centers a single child.
generationHints: Import StyledCycle from 'fluent-styles'.
Pair with StyledIcon for circular icon buttons.
Use size='xl' for user avatar placeholders.
Override backgroundColor for brand-colored circles.

typeDefinitions.name: StyledCycleProps
typeDefinitions.summary: Props for StyledCycle including size variant and children.

typeDefinitions.name: CycleVariants
typeDefinitions.summary: Named size variants for StyledCycle.

records.embeddingText: StyledCycle is a fluent-styles circular container. Size variants sm(32) md(48) lg(64) xl(80). Gray border and background defaults. Used internally by Header. Export confirmed in src/index.ts.
records.rawContent: Component StyledCycle/CycleBase. Category helper. Exported from fluent-styles via src/index.ts. Source: src/package/cycle/index.tsx. Circular container with size variants. Related: StyledIcon, Header.

metadata.notes: Fully verified from source. Size map and defaults confirmed.

records.summary: Visual divider family: Separator (line divider), SeparatorWithLabel (divider with centered text), SeparatorGroup (repeated dividers), DottedSeparator (dotted line). Supports horizontal/vertical orientation and four color variants.
whenToUse: Use Separator to visually divide sections or list items.
Use SeparatorWithLabel for 'OR' dividers in login/signup forms.
Use SeparatorGroup for decorative repeated divider patterns.
Use DottedSeparator for decorative or draft-style section breaks.
whenNotToUse: Do not use Separator as a spacer; use StyledSpacer for spacing.
Do not use a full-width Separator inside a card if the card already has dividers.

props.name: orientation
props.description: Direction of the separator line.

props.name: variant
props.description: Color variant. default=gray[300], muted=gray[200], primary=blue[300], subtle=gray[100].

props.name: color
props.description: Override separator color directly.

props.name: thickness
props.description: Line thickness in pixels.

props.name: width
props.description: Width (horizontal separators). Defaults to full width.

props.name: height
props.description: Height (vertical separators). Defaults to full height.

props.name: flex
props.description: Flex value for use inside flex containers (e.g. flex={1} in SeparatorWithLabel).

props.name: label
props.description: SeparatorWithLabel only: Text centered between the two separator lines.

props.name: labelColor
props.description: SeparatorWithLabel only: Label text color.

props.name: labelSize
props.description: SeparatorWithLabel only: Label font size.

props.name: count
props.description: SeparatorGroup only: Number of separator lines to render.

props.name: spacing
props.description: SeparatorGroup only: Gap between separator lines.

notes: variantConfig maps variants to theme color tokens.

examples.title: Basic horizontal separator
examples.description: Full-width line divider between sections.
examples.code:
import { Separator } from 'fluent-styles';

<Separator />

examples.title: OR separator for login form
examples.description: Text divider between email and social login options.
examples.code:
import { SeparatorWithLabel } from 'fluent-styles';

<SeparatorWithLabel label="OR" variant="muted" />

examples.title: Vertical separator
examples.description: Vertical divider between two columns.
examples.code:
import { Separator, XStack } from 'fluent-styles';

<XStack height={40} alignItems="center">
  <Separator orientation="vertical" />
</XStack>

examples.title: Primary colored separator
examples.description: Blue separator to highlight a section break.
examples.code:
import { Separator } from 'fluent-styles';

<Separator variant="primary" thickness={2} />

bestPractices: Use variant='muted' for subtle list item dividers.
Use SeparatorWithLabel for auth screen 'OR' dividers.
Use flex={1} on Separator inside XStack for auto-expanding lines.
antiPatterns: Do not use Separator as vertical spacing; use StyledSpacer.
Do not set thickness > 2 for standard dividers — it becomes too heavy visually.
generationHints: Import Separator from 'fluent-styles'.
Use <Separator /> for simple full-width horizontal dividers.
Use <SeparatorWithLabel label='OR' /> for auth form dividers.
Use variant='muted' inside lists for soft dividers.
Use variant='primary' for highlighted section breaks.

typeDefinitions.name: SeparatorProps
typeDefinitions.summary: Props for Separator including orientation, variant, thickness, color.

typeDefinitions.name: SeparatorWithLabelProps
typeDefinitions.summary: Adds label, labelColor, labelSize, leftContent, rightContent, gap.

typeDefinitions.name: SeparatorGroupProps
typeDefinitions.summary: Props for rendering count repeated separators with spacing.

records.embeddingText: Separator is a fluent-styles visual divider family. Supports horizontal and vertical orientation. Variants: default(gray[300]), muted(gray[200]), primary(blue[300]), subtle(gray[100]). SeparatorWithLabel for OR dividers. DottedSeparator for dotted lines. Export confirmed in src/index.ts.
records.rawContent: Component Separator/SeparatorWithLabel/SeparatorGroup/DottedSeparator. Category helper. Exported from fluent-styles via src/index.ts. Source: src/package/separator/index.tsx.

metadata.notes: Fully verified from source. Four-member family, variant config, and orientation confirmed.

records.summary: Loading indicator family: Spinner (activity indicator), SpinnerContainer (full-screen modal overlay), InlineSpinner (compact horizontal/vertical spinner with text). Five color variants and three sizes.
whenToUse: Use Spinner for inline loading states within a UI region.
Use SpinnerContainer for full-screen loading overlays (blocking operations).
Use InlineSpinner for compact spinner+text combos inside buttons or list items.
Use overlay={true} on Spinner for a semi-transparent overlay without the SpinnerContainer modal.
whenNotToUse: Do not use SpinnerContainer if the operation is non-blocking; use Spinner.
Do not use Spinner without visual context; always pair with a label or message for accessibility.

props.name: size
props.description: Spinner size. Named: small=30, medium=50, large=80. Accepts raw number.

props.name: variant
props.description: Color variant. primary=blue[500], success=green[500], warning=amber[500], danger=red[500], default=gray[400].

props.name: color
props.description: Override spinner color directly.

props.name: overlay
props.description: Spinner only: When true, renders as a position:absolute semi-transparent overlay.

props.name: overlayColor
props.description: Background color of the overlay.

props.name: label
props.description: Spinner only: Text displayed below the spinner.

props.name: labelColor
props.description: Color of the label text (white[500] when overlay=true).

props.name: labelSize
props.description: Font size of the label.

props.name: accessibilityLabel
props.description: Accessibility label for screen readers.

props.name: isVisible
props.description: SpinnerContainer only: When false, renders nothing.

props.name: backdropColor
props.description: SpinnerContainer only: Background color of the full-screen backdrop.

props.name: message
props.description: SpinnerContainer only: Message text shown below the spinner.

props.name: onBackdropPress
props.description: SpinnerContainer only: Called when the backdrop is pressed (dismissible overlay).

props.name: text
props.description: InlineSpinner only: Text displayed next to the spinner.

props.name: direction
props.description: InlineSpinner only: Layout direction for spinner + text.

props.name: gap
props.description: InlineSpinner only: Gap between spinner and text.

notes: SpinnerContainer has darker backdrop (0.5 alpha) vs Spinner overlay (0.3 alpha).

examples.title: Inline loading spinner
examples.description: Simple centered spinner.
examples.code:
import { Spinner } from 'fluent-styles';

<Spinner size="medium" variant="primary" />

examples.title: Spinner with label
examples.description: Spinner with loading text.
examples.code:
import { Spinner } from 'fluent-styles';

<Spinner
  size="large"
  variant="primary"
  label="Loading data..."
/>

examples.title: Full screen loading overlay
examples.description: Blocking modal spinner with message.
examples.code:
import { SpinnerContainer } from 'fluent-styles';

<SpinnerContainer
  isVisible={isLoading}
  message="Processing your request..."
  variant="primary"
  size="large"
/>

examples.title: Inline spinner in button
examples.description: Compact spinner with text for loading states in compact UI.
examples.code:
import { InlineSpinner } from 'fluent-styles';

<InlineSpinner size="small" text="Saving..." variant="primary" />

examples.title: Dismissible spinner overlay
examples.description: Overlay spinner that can be dismissed.

bestPractices: Use SpinnerContainer for blocking operations that prevent user interaction.
Always set isVisible to hide SpinnerContainer when loading is done.
Use label or message for all spinners to improve accessibility.
Use InlineSpinner with direction='row' for button loading states.
antiPatterns: Do not use SpinnerContainer for non-blocking operations; use Spinner.
Do not omit label/message — provide context for screen readers and users.
generationHints: Import Spinner, SpinnerContainer, or InlineSpinner from 'fluent-styles'.
Use SpinnerContainer for full-screen loading overlays controlled by isVisible.
Use Spinner with overlay={true} for in-screen partial overlays.
Use InlineSpinner for compact loading states inline with other UI.
Default variant is 'primary' (blue); use 'success' after completion feedback.

typeDefinitions.name: SpinnerProps
typeDefinitions.summary: Props for the base Spinner.

typeDefinitions.name: SpinnerContainerProps
typeDefinitions.summary: Adds isVisible, backdropColor, message, onBackdropPress.

typeDefinitions.name: InlineSpinnerProps
typeDefinitions.summary: Adds text, direction, gap.

records.embeddingText: Spinner is a fluent-styles loading indicator family. Spinner is inline. SpinnerContainer is full-screen modal overlay. InlineSpinner is compact row/column spinner with text. Variants: default, primary, success, warning, danger. Sizes: small(30), medium(50), large(80). Export confirmed in src/index.ts.
records.rawContent: Component Spinner/SpinnerContainer/InlineSpinner. Category feedback. Exported from fluent-styles via src/index.ts. Source: src/package/spinner/index.tsx. Three-member family. Related: StyledButton, StyledDialog.

metadata.notes: Fully verified from source. Three-member family, variant config, size map, overlay behavior confirmed.
